title: memory_read fires on non-recall utterances in root mode
what_failed: In root mode, Claude triggered memory_read with queries 'lemme', 'restriction', and 'that' â€” none of which are recall queries. 'lemme' came from 'can you lemme know...', 'restriction' from a question about restricted AI, 'that' from a follow-up. The Step 2 reject list covers filler words like 'followed, planning, going, rn, atm' but not conversational connectors like 'lemme', 'know', 'tell', 'that', 'this', etc. The Step 1 check (is Ash asking me to recall?) is supposed to gate this, but isn't working reliably.
where_failed: prompts/consciousness.txt lines 101-121 (memory_read Query Formation Rules Steps 1-3)
why_likely: The Step 1 decision rule says 'questions about stored info â†’ yes, search' but Claude over-triggers on any interrogative phrasing. The reject list in Step 2 is too short.
suggested_fix: Expand Step 1 with explicit no-search patterns. Expand Step 2 reject list with: lemme, know, tell, said, what, that, this, here, can, could, would, should, is, are, was, were, it, do, did, have, has, had. Add a positive-pattern check: only search if the query word would plausibly appear in a stored memory entry.
